seoTitle: How to Find Starred Templates on Canva Easily
metaDescription: Quickly locate your starred Canva templates so you can reuse favorite designs instead of searching through your entire template library.
intro: If you regularly reuse the same Canva layouts for social posts, presentations, or marketing materials, starring templates saves real time. This guide covers where starred templates live in Canva and how to get back to them quickly, whether you're working solo or coordinating designs across a team.

steps.heading: Star a template while designing
steps.body: When you find a template worth reusing, mark it as a favorite so it's saved separately from Canva's general template library.

steps.heading: Open your starred items
steps.body: Navigate to the section of Canva where favorited or starred content is collected, rather than searching the full catalog each time.

steps.heading: Filter to templates specifically
steps.body: Narrow the view to templates if your starred list also includes other saved elements, so you find the right design faster.

steps.heading: Reuse the template for a new design
steps.body: Open the starred template and start a new project from it, keeping your original saved for future use.

steps.heading: Keep your starred list organized
steps.body: Periodically remove templates you no longer use so the list stays a quick reference rather than another cluttered folder.

faq.question: Where do starred templates show up in Canva?
faq.answer: They're collected in a dedicated favorites area separate from Canva's full template library, so you don't have to search from scratch.

faq.question: Can I unstar a template later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can remove a template from your starred list at any time if you no longer need quick access to it.

faq.question: Is starring templates useful for teams?
faq.answer: Yes, it helps teams quickly return to approved or frequently used layouts instead of rebuilding designs from scratch each time.

faq.question: Does starring a template change the original design?
faq.answer: No, starring just saves a reference to the template for faster access; it doesn't modify the design itself.
